## YARA v0.2.0-alpha.1

### Scope

Pre-alpha Web UI release for bounded, local-only, read-only operator visibility over existing catalog coverage, runtime drift posture, and lifecycle publication readiness evidence.

### Implemented in this tag

- W1: bounded local read-only API server via `yara serve`.
- W2: embedded Web UI shell served by `yara serve --ui`.
- W3: runtime drift posture view with assertion-scoped filtering and fail-closed payload handling.
- W4: lifecycle publication readiness view with assertion-scoped filtering, four-pillar gate status display, and fail-closed payload handling.

### Catalog Version

- `catalog/v0.2/snapshot.yaml` digest: `sha256:f10ba7687982c3e678dcf47b141cbaf42a740c2658018f40cd4756308d2018a1`

### Schema Digest Set

Record exact schema artifact digests from the release build:

- `yara-schemas-v1alpha1.tar.gz`: `<populate-from-checksums.txt>`
- `checksums.txt`: `<populate-after-release-build>`

### Included Release Artifacts

- `yara_<version>_linux_amd64.tar.gz`
- `yara_<version>_linux_arm64.tar.gz`
- `yara_<version>_darwin_arm64.tar.gz`
- `yara-schemas-v1alpha1.tar.gz`
- `checksums.txt`

### Web UI startup

After extracting a release binary and preparing a coverage report:

- `./yara serve --catalog catalog/v0.2/snapshot.yaml --coverage-report .yara/catalog-v0.2-coverage.yaml --ui --port 7474`
- open `http://127.0.0.1:7474`.

### Known limitations

- Web UI is local-only and read-only in pre-alpha (no auth, no multi-user sessions, no mutation endpoints).
- Full clean-cluster installation remains out of scope; bootstrap is limited to one explicit YARA-owned namespace and one model PVC.
- Air-gap completeness remains external for acquisition execution, transfer-medium attestation trust chain, and scanning attestations.
- Integration execution remains bounded contract validation and is not throughput/latency/availability certification.
- Runtime manager, backup/restore, version upgrade, team API, multi-node planning, and RAG topology remain deferred.

### Support boundary

- Supported as an audited pre-alpha evaluation toolchain only.
- No production support or uptime/SLA commitments.
- No implicit resource adoption, prune, delete, or rollback side effects outside explicit bounded commands.
- Operators must treat all release outputs as immutable evidence inputs and verify checksums before use.
